enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for all parsed records; the canonical JSON representation must be identical after shuffling meta-information lines (except ##fileformat which stays first)

evidence.quote: Other than |##fileformat|, they may appear in any order.

enforced.mr_name: Structured meta-line key-value pair order invariance
preconditions: header contains at least one structured ##INFO, ##FORMAT, or ##FILTER line with multiple key=value pairs
enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for all parsed records; implementations must not rely on the order of fields within structured lines

evidence.quote: Implementations must not rely on the order of the fields within structured lines and are not required to preserve field ordering.

enforced.mr_name: ALT allele permutation with consistent remapping of GT, Number=A, and Number=R fields
enforced.oracle: After permuting ALT alleles and consistently remapping GT indices and Number=A/R field values, the parsed variant record must represent the same biological variant set. GT values must be remapped so that allele index 0 still refers to REF, and indices 1..n refer to the reordered ALT alleles. Number=A values must be reordered by the same permutation. Number=R values must keep REF (index 0) fixed and reorder the ALT values by the permutation. The canonical JSON output from parsing x and T(x) must be semantically equivalent.

evidence.quote: The allele values are 0 for the reference allele (what is in the REF field), 1 for the first allele listed in ALT, 2 for the second allele list in ALT and so on.
A: The field has one value per alternate allele. The values must be in the same order as listed in the ALT column (described in section data-lines).
R: The field has one value for each possible allele, including the reference. The order of the values must be the reference allele first, then the alternate alleles as listed in the ALT column.
G: The field has one value for each possible genotype. The values must be in the same order as prescribed in section <ref> (see Genotype Ordering).

enforced.mr_name: Query method invariance under ALT allele permutation
enforced.oracle: After permuting ALT alleles and consistently remapping GT indices and Number=A/R field values, the following query methods on the parsed record must return identical values before and after the transform: getNAlleles() (total allele count unchanged), isBiallelic() (biallelic status unchanged), isVariant() (variant status unchanged), getType() (variant type unchanged), isSNP() (SNP status unchanged). These methods depend only on the biological variant, not on the ordering of ALT alleles.

evidence.quote: The allele values are 0 for the reference allele (what is in the REF field), 1 for the first allele listed in ALT, 2 for the second allele list in ALT and so on.
A: The field has one value per alternate allele. The values must be in the same order as listed in the ALT column (described in section data-lines).
R: The field has one value for each possible allele, including the reference. The order of the values must be the reference allele first, then the alternate alleles as listed in the ALT column.

enforced.mr_name: getStart and getEnd query invariance under meta-line shuffle
enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each listed method. getStart() must equal POS (1-based), getEnd() must equal POS + len(REF) - 1 (1-based inclusive), and getLengthOnReference() must equal len(REF). These are invariant under meta-line reordering.

evidence.quote: * POS — position: The reference position, with the 1st base having position 1.
This is a computed field that, when present, must be set to the maximum end reference position (1-based) of: the position of the final base of the REF allele
The value in the POS field refers to the position of the first base in the String.

enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each listed method. The 1-based POS (getStart), END = POS + len(REF) - 1 (getEnd), and contig (getChr) must be invariant under VCF->BCF->VCF transcoding.

evidence.quote: * POS — position: The reference position, with the 1st base having position 1.
This is a computed field that, when present, must be set to the maximum end reference position (1-based) of: the position of the final base of the REF allele
The value in the POS field refers to the position of the first base in the String.

enforced.mr_name: Meta-information line shuffle invariance under writer round-trip
enforced.oracle: Shuffling meta-information lines (except ##fileformat which stays first) then round-tripping through the SUT writer must produce the same canonical content as the original.

evidence.quote: [VCF v4.5 — Meta-information lines] Other than |##fileformat|, they may appear in any order.
[VCF v4.5 — List of changes] Meta-information lines can be in any order, with the exception of ##fileformat which must come first.

enforced.mr_name: Meta-information line shuffle preserves query method results
enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each listed query method — meta lines (other than ##fileformat) may appear in any order per spec, so shuffling them must not change parsed record query results

evidence.quote:
[VCF v4.5 — Meta-information lines] Meta-information lines are optional, but if they are present then they must be completely well-formed.
Other than |##fileformat|, they may appear in any order.

enforced.mr_name: Structured meta-line key-value order permutation preserves query method results
enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each listed query method — implementations must not rely on the order of fields within structured meta lines

evidence.quote:
[VCF v4.5 — Meta-information lines] To aid human readability, the order of fields should be ID, Number, Type, Description, then any optional fields.
Implementations must not rely on the order of the fields within structured lines and are not required to preserve field ordering.

enforced.mr_name: Sample column permutation preserves per-sample query method results
enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each listed query method — sample columns are an arbitrary set of IDs; permuting them preserves all per-sample aggregate counts

evidence.quote:
[VCF v4.5 — Header line syntax] If genotype data is present in the file, these are followed by a FORMAT column header, then an arbitrary number of sample IDs.
Duplicate sample IDs are not allowed.

quarantine.mr_name: INFO field key-value pair order invariance
preconditions: at least one data record has multiple INFO subfields (e.g. DP=30;AF=0.5;MQ=60)
quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for all parsed records; the INFO field is a semicolon-separated series of key=value pairs with no specified ordering semantics

evidence.quote: INFO — additional information: Semicolon-separated series of additional information fields, or the MISSING value `.' if none are present.

ambiguity_flags: The spec does not explicitly state that INFO key order is semantically irrelevant, but it defines INFO as a 'series' without ordering constraints, and no spec rule mandates a particular order

preconditions: at least 2 sample columns in the header
quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) after re-indexing sample IDs; the same set of sample genotypes is present, just in a different column order

evidence.quote: Other than |##fileformat|, they may appear in any order.

ambiguity_flags: The spec does not explicitly state that sample column order is semantically irrelevant; however, sample IDs are named and parsers typically access them by ID, not column index

quarantine.mr_name: BCF header dictionary order invariance via round-trip
quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) after BCF round-trip; dictionary entries are re-indexed by the BCF codec, so the order of ##contig, ##INFO, ##FORMAT, ##FILTER declarations must not affect the canonical output

evidence.quote: Other than |##fileformat|, they may appear in any order.

ambiguity_flags: BCF dictionary ordering is implementation-defined per VCF v4.5 §6.2.1; the IDX field can override implicit ordering

quarantine.oracle: The trimmed representation (REF without shared prefix/suffix, ALT without shared prefix/suffix, POS adjusted) must be recognized as the same variant by a normalization-aware parser. Parsing the original and trimmed forms must yield identical variant semantics (same genomic position and allele content after normalization).

evidence.quote: For simple insertions and deletions in which either the REF or one of the ALT alleles would otherwise be null/empty, the REF and ALT Strings must include the base before the variant (which must be reflected in the POS field), unless the variant occurs at position 1 on the contig in which case it must include the base after the variant; this padding base is not required (although it is permitted) e.g. for complex substitutions or other variants where all alleles have at least one base represented in their Strings.
ALT --- alternate base(s): Comma-separated list of alternate non-reference alleles.

ambiguity_flags: The spec says padding base is 'not required (although it is permitted)' for complex substitutions — this means both padded and unpadded forms are valid, but a parser that does not normalize may treat them as distinct variants.

quarantine.mr_name: Left-alignment invariance for indels in homopolymer runs
quarantine.oracle: The left-shifted indel (POS decremented by 1, first base preserved) must be recognized as the same variant as the original. Parsing both forms must yield identical variant semantics (same genomic position and allele content after normalization per Tan 2015 §2.1).

evidence.quote: For simple insertions and deletions in which either the REF or one of the ALT alleles would otherwise be null/empty, the REF and ALT Strings must include the base before the variant (which must be reflected in the POS field), unless the variant occurs at position 1 on the contig in which case it must include the base after the variant; this padding base is not required (although it is permitted) e.g. for complex substitutions or other variants where all alleles have at least one base represented in their Strings.

ambiguity_flags: The spec does not explicitly mandate left-alignment; it only describes padding base requirements. Left-alignment is a best practice from Tan 2015, not a VCF spec requirement per se.

quarantine.oracle: Splitting a multi-ALT record into one record per ALT allele (with synchronized Number=A INFO/FORMAT arrays and remapped GT indices) must produce the same set of variants. Parsing the multi-ALT form and the split form must yield identical variant semantics — the same alleles at the same position, with per-sample genotypes correctly remapped.

evidence.quote: R: The field has one value for each possible allele, including the reference. The order of the values must be the reference allele first, then the alternate alleles as listed in the ALT column.
A: The field has one value per alternate allele. The values must be in the same order as listed in the ALT column (described in section <ref>).

A: The field has one value per alternate allele. The values must be in the same order as listed in the ALT column (described in section data-lines). R: The field has one value for each possible allele, including the reference. The order of the values must be the reference allele first, then the alternate alleles as listed in the ALT column.

ambiguity_flags: The VCF spec does not explicitly define a multi-allelic split operation; this is a convention from bcftools norm. The spec mandates Number=A and Number=R ordering relative to ALT, which split_multi_allelic preserves.

quarantine.mr_name: Query method invariance under common-affix trimming
quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each listed method. After trimming common affixes, the variant's allele count, start position (adjusted), end position, length on reference, and variant type classification must remain consistent.

evidence.quote: For simple insertions and deletions in which either the REF or one of the ALT alleles would otherwise be null/empty, the REF and ALT Strings must include the base before the variant (which must be reflected in the POS field), unless the variant occurs at position 1 on the contig in which case it must include the base after the variant; this padding base is not required (although it is permitted) e.g. for complex substitutions or other variants where all alleles have at least one base represented in their Strings.

ambiguity_flags: The spec says padding base is 'not required (although it is permitted)' — trimmed vs untrimmed forms are both valid but may produce different getStart/getEnd values if the parser does not normalize.

quarantine.mr_name: Telomeric boundary coordinate invariance (POS=0 or POS=N+1) under round-trip
quarantine.oracle: Records with POS=0 (left telomere) or POS=N+1 (right telomere, where N is contig length) must survive a write-roundtrip with identical POS values. The spec explicitly permits these boundary positions.

evidence.quote: Telomeres are indicated by using positions 0 or N+1, where N is the length of the corresponding chromosome or contig.
Telomeres are indicated by using positions 0 or N+1, where N is the length of the corresponding chromosome or contig.

ambiguity_flags: The spec permits but does not require parsers to support telomeric positions 0 and N+1; some parsers may reject or silently modify these boundary coordinates.

quarantine.mr_name: VCF-BCF codec round-trip preserves all variant information
quarantine.oracle: Parsing the original VCF and parsing the VCF recovered from BCF round-trip must yield identical canonical content (same records, same INFO/FORMAT values, same genotypes).

evidence.quote: [VCF v4.5 — BCF specification] §.§ Encoding a VCF record example
[VCF v4.5 — BCF2 records] §.§ Encoding a VCF record example

quarantine.mr_name: Sample column permutation invariance under writer round-trip
quarantine.oracle: Permuting sample column order across the entire file and round-tripping through the SUT writer must produce the same canonical content (sample IDs and data columns permuted identically).

evidence.quote:
[VCF v4.5 — Header line syntax] #CHROM
    POS
    ID
    REF
    ALT
    QUAL
    FILTER
    INFO
[VCF v4.5 — Data lines] #CHROM     POS     ID     REF     ALT     QUAL     FILTER     INFO     FORMAT     SAMPLE1

ambiguity_flags: Sample column order is not explicitly specified as semantically meaningful; the spec defines the fixed columns but sample columns after FORMAT are implementation-defined in ordering.

quarantine.mr_name: INFO field key-value shuffle invariance under writer round-trip
quarantine.oracle: Shuffling the semicolon-separated key=value pairs in the INFO column and round-tripping through the SUT writer must produce the same canonical content, as key-value order is not semantically meaningful.

evidence.quote: [VCF v4.5 — Information field format] * R: The field has one value for each possible allele, including the reference. The order of the values must be the reference allele first, then the alternate alleles as listed in the ALT column.

ambiguity_flags: The VCF spec does not explicitly state that INFO key=value pairs within a semicolon-separated list may appear in any order, though this is conventional.

quarantine.mr_name: Missing-value injection invariance under writer round-trip
quarantine.oracle: Injecting a FORMAT field filled with '.' (missing values) for all samples is semantically a no-op; round-tripping through the SUT writer must preserve the original variant content.

evidence.quote: [VCF v4.5 — Meta-information lines] It is recommended to include meta-information lines describing the entries used in the body of the VCF file.

ambiguity_flags: The spec recommends but does not require that all FORMAT fields used in data be declared in meta-information lines.

quarantine.mr_name: INFO field key-value shuffle preserves query method results
quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each listed query method — shuffling semicolon-separated INFO key=value pairs is semantically a no-op

evidence.quote:
[VCF v4.5 — Meta-information lines] Meta-information lines are optional, but if they are present then they must be completely well-formed.
Other than |##fileformat|, they may appear in any order.

ambiguity_flags: The spec does not explicitly state that INFO field key-value ordering is semantically irrelevant, but by analogy with meta-line ordering and structured field ordering, implementations should not depend on it
